title: Dedicatory Poem to the Princess Alice
content:
Dead Princess, living Power, if that which lived
True life live onï¿½and if the fatal kiss,
Born of true life and love, divorce thee not
From earthly love and lifeï¿½if what we call
The spirit flash not all at once from out
This shadow into Substanceï¿½then perhaps
The mellowï¿½d murmur of the peopleï¿½s praise
From thine own State, and all our breadth of realm,
Where Love and Longing dress thy deeds in light,
Ascends to thee; and this March morn that sees
Thy Soldier-brotherï¿½s bridal orange-bloom
Break throï¿½ the yews and cypress of thy grave,
And thine Imperial mother smile again,
May send one ray to thee! and who can tellï¿½
Thouï¿½Englandï¿½s England-loving daughterï¿½thou
Dying so English thou wouldst have her flag
Borne on thy coffinï¿½where is he can swear
But that some broken gleam from our poor earth
May touch thee, while, remembering thee, I lay
At thy pale feet this ballad of the deeds
Of England, and her banner in the East?
meaning:
"Dedicatory Poem to the Princess Alice" is a quintessential example of Alfred Lord Tennyson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
